SEARCH
Package timgutilities.textio

Class TextIOUtils

java.lang.Object
timgutilities.textio.TextIOUtils
public class TextIOUtils extends Object
This class represents a bunch of utility functions that over the years I have found myself having to repeatedly write, so I basically took my usual code and put it here. Most of the code here is around the reading and validation of input data. However there is also a mechanism to use that code to then run a command structure allowing you to navigate between different levels of a command tree and on selection of a command to execute Java code (I recommend calling lambda) to do the actual processing. I've mostly used this for putting together simple test code before it's then converted into a proper client.

    static timgutilities.textio.DirectoryEntry
    choseFromDirectory(String prompt, String dir, timgutilities.textio.DirectorySelectionMode selectionMode, timgutilities.textio.DirectoryListOrderType listOrderType, boolean excludeHiddenFiles, String regexp, boolean regexpFilesOnly, boolean nameAndType, boolean addAbandon)
    From the given directory list the directory entries as strings subject to the controls Entries shown will depend on the selectionMode, so if the selection mode limits choices to directories only directories will be shown, but a mode that allows only files to be chosen will display the directories for navigation purposes, but they cannot be chosen.

    static ZoneOffset
    getISOOffsetTimeZoneOffset(String prompt, boolean defaulToCurrentOffset)
    prompts for an offset from GMT in terms of hours an mins, the resulting string will be 01:00 (in the case of british summer time which is 1 hours ahead of GMT, or 04:30) for India which is 4 1/2 hours ahead.

  • Method Details

    • setDefaultPauseString

      public static void setDefaultPauseString(String newDefaultPauseString)
      Lets you set the default string to be displayed when pausing
      Parameters:
      newDefaultPauseString - the new string

    • getDefaultPauseString

      public static String getDefaultPauseString()
      gets the default pause string
      Returns:
      the current default pause string

    • doOutput

      public static void doOutput(String output)
      Output text in a manner that is compatible with prompts from the input routines
      Parameters:
      output - the text to output

    • pauseBeforeProgressing

      public static void pauseBeforeProgressing() throws IOException
      Waits for the user to press return - useful if you need to allow for something in a separate environment to complete (e.g. the provisioning of a service in a cloud) Displays the defaultPauseString to let the user know they need to do something
      Throws:
      IOException - if there is a problem setting up the reader on the input or reading the input

    • pauseBeforeProgressing

      public static void pauseBeforeProgressing(String prompt) throws IOException
      Displays the prompt and then waits for the user tp press return - useful if you need to allow for something in a separate environment to complete (e.g. the provisioning of a service in a cloud)
      Parameters:
      prompt - the prompt to use when asking for input
      Throws:
      IOException - if there is a problem setting up the reader on the input or reading the input

    • getString

      public static String getString(String prompt) throws IOException
      Outputs the prompt and then waits for the user to enter zero or more characters which are then returned.
      Parameters:
      prompt - the prompt to use when asking for input
      Returns:
      the text that was input
      Throws:
      IOException - if there is a problem setting up the reader on the input or reading the input

    • getString

      public static String getString(String prompt, String defaultValue) throws IOException
      Displays the provided prompt and asks for input. If the defaultValue is non null that is diplayed and entering nothing (e.g. just pressing rreturn) will return the default value, if there is not default value provided pressing return results in a notice that input is required
      Parameters:
      prompt - the prompt to use when asking for input
      defaultValue - a default value to return if the user just presses return
      Returns:
      the entered text or the defaultValue if they just pressed return
      Throws:
      IOException - if there is a problem setting up the reader on the input or reading the input

    • getString

      public static String getString(String prompt, String defaultValue, boolean allowEmptyInput) throws IOException
      Displays the provided prompt and asks for input. If the defaultValue is non null that is displayed and entering nothing (e.g. just pressing return) will return the default value given, if there is not default value and allowEmptyInput is false then pressing return results in a notice that input is required, if allowEmptyInput is true and the user just presses return the the empty string is returned
      Parameters:
      prompt - the prompt to use when asking for input
      defaultValue - a default value to return if the user just presses return
      allowEmptyInput - if true and there is no default if the user presses return the method will return an empty string, if false and there is no default the users is prompted to enter input
      Returns:
      the entered text or the defaultValue / empty string if they just pressed return
      Throws:
      IOException - if there is a problem setting up the reader on the input or reading the input

    • getYN

      public static boolean getYN(String prompt, boolean defaultValue) throws IOException
      Displays the prompt and asks the user to enter y or n (case insensitive) if y is entered then true is returned, if n then false, if the input is neither y or n then the user is re-asked for input. If the input is empty then the defaultValue is returned
      Parameters:
      prompt - the prompt to use when asking for input
      defaultValue - a default value to return if the user just presses return
      Returns:
      the boolean value of true if they entered y/Y and false if they entered n/N or the default if they just pressed return
      Throws:
      IOException - if there is a problem setting up the reader on the input or reading the input

    • getYN

      public static boolean getYN(String prompt) throws IOException
      Displays the prompt and asks the user to enter y or n (case insensitive) if y is entered then true is returned, if n then false, if the input is neither y or n then the user is re-asked for input.
      Parameters:
      prompt - the prompt to use when asking for input
      Returns:
      the boolean value of true if they entered y/Y and false if they entered n/N
      Throws:
      IOException - if there is a problem setting up the reader on the input or reading the input

    • selectAndRunLoop

      public static void selectAndRunLoop(String prompt, timgutilities.textio.RunnableCommand[] runableCommands, boolean insertQuit) throws IOException
      display the prompt, then a list of command names strings from the entries in the RunnableCommands, if insertQuit is present then a quit option is added as the first option. The user is prompted to enter a number representing the command they want to run, if the quit option is chosen (see insertQuit above) then the user is asked to confirm they want to quit the loop and the method will return. The RunnableCommands runnable is called. If the runnable throws an exception it's trapped and displayed as output. Once the runnable command has been completed the text output it returns is displayed if it is non null. if the runnable returns null then nothing is output Once the output (if any) is presented then provided the chosen option was not the quit option (if that option is displayed - see insertQuit above) the sequence returns to the start and the options are re-output and the loop continues. If the user chooses the quit option they will be asked to confirm before this method exits the loop and returns
      Parameters:
      prompt - the prompt to use when asking for input
      runableCommands - the list of commands to offer
      insertQuit - if true a quit option will be added
      Throws:
      IOException - if there is a problem setting up the reader on the input or reading the input

    • selectAndRunLoop

      public static void selectAndRunLoop(String prompt, timgutilities.textio.RunnableCommand[] runableCommands, boolean insertQuit, boolean confirmQuit) throws IOException
      display the prompt, then a list of command names strings from the entries in the RunnableCommands, if insertQuit is present then a quit option is added as the first option. The user is prompted to enter a number representing the command they want to run, if the quit option is chosen (see insertQuit above) then if confirmQuit is true the user is asked to confirm they want to quit the loop and the method will return, if confirmQuit is false the loop will exit withuth asking for confirmation. The RunnableCommands runnable is called. If the runnable throws an exception it's trapped and displayed as output. Once the runnable command has been completed the text output it returns is displayed if it is non null. if the runnable returns null then nothing is output Once the output (if any) is presented then provided the chosen option was not the quit option (if that option is displayed - see insertQuit above) the sequence returns to the start and the options are re-output and the loop continues. If the user chooses the quit option and confirmQuit is true they will be asked to confirm before this method exits the loop and returns
      Parameters:
      prompt - the prompt to use when asking for input
      runableCommands - the list of commands to offer
      insertQuit - if true a quit option will be added
      confirmQuit - if true then the user has to confirm the quit
      Throws:
      IOException - if there is a problem setting up the reader on the input or reading the input

    • selectAndRunLoop

      public static void selectAndRunLoop(String prompt, timgutilities.textio.RunnableCommand[] runableCommands, boolean insertQuit, boolean confirmQuit, boolean quitFirst) throws IOException
      display the prompt, then a list of command names strings from the entries in the RunnableCommands, if insertQuit is present then if quitFirst is present a quit option is added as the first option, if quitFirst is false then the quit option will be the last option. The user is prompted to enter a number representing the command they want to run, if the quit option is chosen (see insertQuit above) then if confirmQuit is true the user is asked to confirm they want to quit the loop and the method will return, if confirmQuit is false the loop will exit withuth asking for confirmation. The RunnableCommands runnable is called. If the runnable throws an exception it's trapped and displayed as output. Once the runnable command has been completed the text output it returns is displayed if it is non null. if the runnable returns null then nothing is output Once the output (if any) is presented then provided the chosen option was not the quit option (if that option is displayed - see insertQuit above) the sequence returns to the start and the options are re-output and the loop continues. If the user chooses the quit option and confirmQuit is true they will be asked to confirm before this method exits the loop and returns
      Parameters:
      prompt - the prompt to use when asking for input
      runableCommands - the list of commands to offer
      insertQuit - if true a quit option will be added
      confirmQuit - if true then the user has to confirm the quit
      quitFirst - if true then the quit option will be inserted at the start of the commands list (option 0) if false it will be at the end.
      Throws:
      IOException - if there is a problem setting up the reader on the input or reading the input

    • getIntChoice

      public static int getIntChoice(String prompt, timgutilities.textio.ChoiceDescriptionData<?> choiceDescriptionData) throws IOException
      Displays the prompt, then the strings in the choice description data entries. The user is prompted to enter an integer representing the choice which it then returned to the caller if it represents one of the choices.
      Parameters:
      prompt - the prompt to use when asking for input
      choiceDescriptionData - the options to be presented, if there is only one item in the choiceDescriptionData then it will be automatically selected
      Returns:
      the number of the chosen option, note that it's up to the caller to detect if this is a quit or separator
      Throws:
      IOException - if there is a problem setting up the reader on the input or reading the input

    • getStringChoice

      public static String getStringChoice(String prompt, String[] options) throws IOException
      Displays the prompt, then the strings in the options array. The user is prompted to enter an integer representing the choice which is then returned to the caller if it represents one of the choices.
      Parameters:
      prompt - the prompt to use when asking for input
      options - the options to be presented, if there is only one item in the options then it will be automatically selected
      Returns:
      the chosen String
      Throws:
      IOException - if there is a problem setting up the reader on the input or reading the input

    • getStringChoice

      public static String getStringChoice(String prompt, String[] options, String defaultValue) throws IOException
      Displays the prompt, then the strings in the options array. The user is prompted to enter an integer representing the choice which is then returned to the caller if it represents one of the choices. if defaultValue is non null AND is one of the options then the user can just press return instead of entering a number to select it
      Parameters:
      prompt - the prompt to use when asking for input
      options - the options to be presented, if there is only one item in the options then it will be automatically selected
      defaultValue - the default value (which must be one of the options) if the user just presses return
      Returns:
      the chosen String
      Throws:
      IOException - if there is a problem setting up the reader on the input or reading the input

    • getStringChoice

      public static String getStringChoice(String prompt, String[] options, Integer defaultIndex) throws IOException
      Displays the prompt, then the strings in the options array. The user is prompted to enter an integer representing the choice which is then returned to the caller if it represents one of the choices. if defaultIndex is between 0 and the mad options size then the user can just press return instead of entering a number to select it
      Parameters:
      prompt - the prompt to use when asking for input
      options - the options to be presented, if there is only one item in the options then it will be automatically selected
      defaultIndex - the index of the default option if the user just presses return
      Returns:
      the chosen String
      Throws:
      IOException - if there is a problem setting up the reader on the input or reading the input

    • getIntChoice

      public static int getIntChoice(String prompt, String[] options) throws IOException
      Displays the prompt, then the strings in the options array. The user is prompted to enter an integer representing the choice which is then returned to the caller if it represents one of the choices.
      Parameters:
      prompt - the prompt to use when asking for input
      options - the options to be presented, if there is only one item in the options then it will be automatically selected
      Returns:
      the index of the chosen option
      Throws:
      IOException - if there is a problem setting up the reader on the input or reading the input

    • getIntChoice

      public static int getIntChoice(String prompt, Collection<String> options) throws IOException
      Displays the prompt, then the strings in the options collection. The user is prompted to enter an integer representing the choice which is then returned to the caller if it represents one of the choices.
      Parameters:
      prompt - the prompt to use when asking for input
      options - the options to be presented, if there is only one item in the options then it will be automatically selected
      Returns:
      the index of the chosen option
      Throws:
      IOException - if there is a problem setting up the reader on the input or reading the input

    • getIntChoice

      public static int getIntChoice(String prompt, Collection<String> options, String defaultValue) throws IOException
      Displays the prompt, then the strings in the options collection. The user is prompted to enter an integer representing the choice which is then returned to the caller if it represents one of the choices.
      Parameters:
      prompt - the prompt to use when asking for input
      options - the options to be presented, if there is only one item in the options then it will be automatically selected
      defaultValue - the default value (which must be one of the options) if the user just presses return
      Returns:
      the index of the chosen option
      Throws:
      IOException - if there is a problem setting up the reader on the input or reading the input

    • getIntChoice

      public static int getIntChoice(String prompt, String[] options, String defaultValue) throws IOException
      Displays the prompt, then the strings in the options array. The user is prompted to enter an integer representing the choice which is then returned to the caller if it represents one of the choices.
      Parameters:
      prompt - the prompt to use when asking for input
      options - the options to be presented, if there is only one item in the options then it will be automatically selected
      defaultValue - the default value (which must be one of the options) if the user just presses return
      Returns:
      the index of the chosen option
      Throws:
      IOException - if there is a problem setting up the reader on the input or reading the input

    • getStringChoice

      public static String getStringChoice(String prompt, timgutilities.textio.ChoiceDescriptionData<?> choiceDescriptionData) throws IOException
      Displays the prompt, then the strings in the choice description data entries. The user is prompted to enter an integer representing the choice, if it represents one of the choices then the string for that choice is returned.
      Parameters:
      prompt - the prompt to use when asking for input
      choiceDescriptionData - the set of choices to present, if there is only one option it will be automatically selected
      Returns:
      the String provided as the option value for the choice.
      Throws:
      IOException - if there is a problem setting up the reader on the input or reading the input

    • getStringChoice

      public static String getStringChoice(String prompt, Collection<String> options) throws IOException
      Displays the prompt, then the strings in the options collection. The user is prompted to enter an integer representing the choice, if it represents one of the choices then the string for that choice is returned.
      Parameters:
      prompt - the prompt to use when asking for input
      options - the set of choices to present, if there is only one option it will be automatically selected
      Returns:
      the String provided as the option value for the choice.
      Throws:
      IOException - if there is a problem setting up the reader on the input or reading the input

    • getStringChoice

      public static String getStringChoice(String prompt, Collection<String> options, String defaultValue) throws IOException
      Displays the prompt, then the strings in the options collection. The user is prompted to enter an integer representing the choice, if it represents one of the choices then the string for that choice is returned. If the defaultValue is not null and is one of the options then it will be marked as the default and the user can just press return to select it
      Parameters:
      prompt - the prompt to use when asking for input
      options - the set of choices to present, if there is only one option it will be automatically selected
      defaultValue - the default value (which must be one of the options) if the user just presses return
      Returns:
      the String provided as the option value for the choice.
      Throws:
      IOException - if there is a problem setting up the reader on the input or reading the input

    • getStringChoice

      public static String getStringChoice(String prompt, Collection<String> options, Integer defaultValue) throws IOException
      Displays the prompt, then the strings in the options collection. The user is prompted to enter an integer representing the choice, if it represents one of the choices then the string for that choice is returned. If the defaultValue is not null and is one of the options then it will be marked as the default and the user can just press return to select it
      Parameters:
      prompt - the prompt to use when asking for input
      options - the set of choices to present, if there is only one option it will be automatically selected
      defaultValue - the index of the default option if the user just presses return
      Returns:
      the String provided as the option value for the choice.
      Throws:
      IOException - if there is a problem setting up the reader on the input or reading the input

    • getParamChoice

      public static <T> T getParamChoice(String prompt, timgutilities.textio.ChoiceDescriptionData<T> choiceDescriptionData) throws IOException
      Displays the choices in the choice description data, asks the user to enter a number representing the choice. If the choice is the "abandon" option (See ChoiceDescriptionData.addAbandion methods) then null is returned, otherwise returns the object in the parameter for the individual choice descriptions
      Type Parameters:
      T - the type of the param object in the choices
      Parameters:
      prompt - the prompt to use when asking for input
      choiceDescriptionData - the set of choices to present, if there is only one option it will be automatically selected
      Returns:
      the object provided as the param value for the choice.
      Throws:
      IOException - if there is a problem setting up the reader on the input or reading the input

    • getString

      public static String getString(String prompt, String[] options) throws IOException
      List the options and ask the user to enter a number representing the option they want, the string selected is returned
      Parameters:
      prompt - the prompt to use when asking for input
      options - the choices
      Returns:
      the chosen option
      Throws:
      IOException - if there is a problem setting up the reader on the input or reading the input

    • getInt

      public static int getInt(String prompt) throws IOException
      Asks the user to enter an integer in base 10, this is parsed and if that fails then the user is re-prompted, if the parsing succeeds then the entered number is returned to the caller
      Parameters:
      prompt - the prompt to use when asking for input
      Returns:
      the entered number
      Throws:
      IOException - if there is a problem setting up the reader on the input or reading the input

    • getIntHex

      public static int getIntHex(String prompt) throws IOException
      Asks the user to enter an integer in base 16 (hex), this is parsed and if that fails then the user is re-prompted, if the parsing succeeds then the entered number is returned to the caller
      Parameters:
      prompt - the prompt to use when asking for input
      Returns:
      the entered number
      Throws:
      IOException - if there is a problem setting up the reader on the input or reading the input

    • getIntBase

      public static int getIntBase(String prompt, int base) throws IOException
      Asks the user to enter an integer in an arbitrary base, this is parsed and if that fails then the user is re-prompted, if the parsing succeeds then the entered number is returned to the caller
      Parameters:
      prompt - the prompt to use when asking for input
      base - the number base to use (e.g. 2 for binary, 16 for hex)
      Returns:
      the entered number
      Throws:
      IOException - if there is a problem setting up the reader on the input or reading the input

    • getInt

      public static int getInt(String prompt, int defaultValue) throws IOException
      Asks the user to enter an integer in base 10, this is parsed and if that fails then the user is re-prompted, if the parsing succeeds then the entered number is returned to the caller. If the user just presses return (no input) then the defaultValue is returned
      Parameters:
      prompt - the prompt to use when asking for input
      defaultValue - the default value to use if the user just presses return
      Returns:
      the entered number
      Throws:
      IOException - if there is a problem setting up the reader on the input or reading the input

    • getIntHex

      public static int getIntHex(String prompt, int defaultValue) throws IOException
      Asks the user to enter an integer in base 16 (hex), this is parsed and if that fails then the user is re-prompted, if the parsing succeeds then the entered number is returned to the caller. If the suer just presses return (no input) then the defaultValue is returned
      Parameters:
      prompt - the prompt to use when asking for input
      defaultValue - the default value to use if the user just presses return
      Returns:
      the entered number
      Throws:
      IOException - if there is a problem setting up the reader on the input or reading the input

      public static int getIntBase(String prompt, int defaultValue, int base) throws IOException
      Asks the user to enter an integer in an arbitrary base, this is parsed and if that fails then the user is re-prompted, if the parsing succeeds then the entered number is returned to the caller. If the user just presses return (no input) then the defaultValue is returned
      Parameters:
      prompt - the prompt to use when asking for input
      defaultValue - the default value to use if the user just presses return
      base - the number base to use (e.g. 2 for binary, 16 for hex)
      Returns:
      the entered number
      Throws:
      IOException - if there is a problem setting up the reader on the input or reading the input

    • getInt

      @Deprecated(forRemoval=true, since="1.0.16") public static int getInt(String prompt, TextIOUtils.NUM_TYPE type, int lower, int upper, boolean useDefault, int defaultValue) throws IOException
      Deprecated, for removal: This API element is subject to removal in a future version.
      Prompt the user to enter a base 10 integer number relative to the upper / lower, if no number is entered then the default value is used. If useDefault is true then the value of the default is displayed after the text describing the restriction, if false it is not This method is deprecated and is only provided for backwards compatibility it is a wrapper around getInt(String , NumberInputOption , int , int, boolean, int) throws IOException This method will be maintained until at least version 2 of this package
      Parameters:
      prompt - the prompt to use when asking for input
      type - the type of checking to do see TextIOUtils.NUM_TYPE
      lower - if relevant for the type the lower limit for checking
      upper - if relevant for the type the upper limit for checking
      useDefault - if true offer the defaultValue
      defaultValue - the default value to use if the user just presses return
      Returns:
      the entered number
      Throws:
      IOException - if there is a problem setting up the reader on the input or reading the input
      See Also:
      • TextIOUtils.NUM_TYPE

    • getInt

      the entered number

      Asks the user to enter a long value and returns a Long
      Parameters:

      Returns:

      Asks the user to enter a hexadecimal value and returns it as a long
      Parameters:
      prompt - the prompt to use when asking for input
      Returns:
      the entered number

      IOException - if there is a problem setting up the reader on the input or reading the input

    • getLongBase

      public static long getLongBase(String prompt, int base) throws IOException
      Asks the user to enter a number in an arbitrary base (specified by the base param) and returns it as a long
      Parameters:
      prompt - the prompt to use when asking for input
      base - the number base to use (e.g. 2 for binary, 16 for hex)
      Returns:
      the entered number
      Throws:
      IOException - if there is a problem setting up the reader on the input or reading the input

    • getLong

      public static long getLong(String prompt, long defaultValue) throws IOException
      Asks the user to enter a long value, but if they press return with no input returns the default
      Parameters:
      prompt - the prompt to use when asking for input
      defaultValue - the default value to use if the user just presses return
      Returns:
      the entered number
      Throws:
      IOException - if there is a problem setting up the reader on the input or reading the input

    • getLongHex

      public static long getLongHex(String prompt, long defaultValue) throws IOException
      Asks the user to enter a hex value and returns as a long, but if they just press return returns the default
      Parameters:
      prompt - the prompt to use when asking for input
      defaultValue - the default value to use if the user just presses return
      Returns:
      the entered number
      Throws:
      IOException - if there is a problem setting up the reader on the input or reading the input

    • getLongBase

      public static long getLongBase(String prompt, long defaultValue, int base) throws IOException
      Asks the user to enter a number in an arbitrary base (specified by the base param) and returns it as a long
      Parameters:
      prompt - the prompt to use when asking for input
      defaultValue - the default value to use if the user just presses return
      base - the base to be used when parsing the input, e.g. 2 for binary, 9 for octal, 10 for decimal, 16 for hex etc.
      Returns:
      the entered number
      Throws:
      IOException - if there is a problem setting up the reader on the input or reading the input

      see getInt(prompt, type, lower, upper, base) but returns a Long This method is deprecated and is only provided for backwards compatibility it is a wrapper around getLongBase(String , NumberInputOption , long , long, int) throws IOException This method will be maintained until at least version 2 of this package
      Parameters:
      prompt - the prompt to use when asking for input
      type - the type of checking to do see TextIOUtils.NUM_TYPE
      lower - if relevant for the type the lower limit for checking
      upper - if relevant for the type the upper limit for checking
      base - the number base to use (e.g. 2 for binary, 16 for hex)
      Returns:
      the entered number
      Throws:
      IOException - if there is a problem setting up the reader on the input or reading the input
      See Also:
      • TextIOUtils.NUM_TYPE

      lower - if relevant for the type the lower limit for checking
      upper - if relevant for the type the upper limit for checking
      defaultValue - the default value to use if the user just presses return
      base - the number base to use (e.g. 2 for binary, 16 for hex)

      the entered number

    • getFile

      public static String getFile(String prompt) throws IOException
      Asks the user to enter a string representing a file name and path which can be relative or absolute, ensures that the entered string represents an existing file (not a directory) if it's not a file then requests another go
      Parameters:
      prompt - the prompt to use when asking for input
      Returns:
      the entered string this will represent a file
      Throws:
      IOException - if there is a problem setting up the reader on the input or reading the input

    • getFile

      public static String getFile(String prompt, String defaultValue) throws IOException
      Asks the user to enter a string representing a file name and path which can be relative or absolute, ensures that the entered string represents an existing file (not a directory) if it's not a file then requests another go, if the user enters and empty string then the defaultValue is used
      Parameters:
      prompt - the prompt to use when asking for input
      defaultValue - the default name to use if the user just presses return
      Returns:
      the entered string this will represent a file
      Throws:
      IOException - if there is a problem setting up the reader on the input or reading the input

    • getFileUnder

      public static String getFileUnder(String prompt, String startLocation) throws IOException
      Asks the user to enter a string representing a file name and path which must be relative to the starting location, ensures that the entered string represents an existing file (not a directory) if it's not a file then requests another go
      Parameters:
      prompt - the prompt to use when asking for input
      startLocation - the directory to prepend to the entered name
      Returns:
      the entered string this will represent a file
      Throws:
      IOException - if there is a problem setting up the reader on the input or reading the input

    • getFileUnder

      public static String getFileUnder(String prompt, String startLocation, String defaultValue) throws IOException
      Asks the user to enter a string representing a file name and path which must be relative to the starting location, ensures that the entered string represents an existing file (not a directory) if it's not a file then requests another go, if the user enters and empty string then the defaultValue is used
      Parameters:
      prompt - the prompt to use when asking for input
      startLocation - the directory to prepend to the entered name
      defaultValue - the default file name if the user just presses return
      Returns:
      the entered string this will represent a file
      Throws:
      IOException - if there is a problem setting up the reader on the input or reading the input

    • getFileMatching

      public static String getFileMatching(String prompt, String regexp) throws IOException
      Asks the user to enter a string representing a file name and path which can be absolute ot relative to the starting location where the filename element (last part of the path) must match the regular expression, ensures that the entered string represents an existing file (not a directory) if it's not a file then requests another go
      Parameters:
      prompt - the prompt to use when asking for input
      regexp - a regular expression that the entered filename must match, if it doesn't then the users is asked to re-enter the name. If null any entered name will match
      Returns:
      the entered string this will represent a file
      Throws:
      IOException - if there is a problem setting up the reader on the input or reading the input

    • getFileMatching

      public static String getFileMatching(String prompt, String regexp, String defaultValue) throws IOException
      Asks the user to enter a string representing a file name and path which can be absolute or relative to the starting location where the filename element (last part of the path) must match the regular expression, ensures that the entered string represents an existing file (not a directory) if it's not a file then requests another go, if the user enters and empty string then the defaultValue is used. Note that if regexp is non null it is only applied to the filename component, not the rest of the path entered
      Parameters:
      prompt - the prompt to use when asking for input
      regexp - if non null then the filename must match this
      defaultValue - the default name if the user just presses return
      Returns:
      the entered string this will represent a file
      Throws:
      IOException - if there is a problem setting up the reader on the input or reading the input

    • getDirectory

      public static String getDirectory(String prompt) throws IOException
      Asks the user to enter a string representing a directory which can be relative or absolute, ensures that the entered string represents an existing directory (not a file) and if it's not then requests another go
      Parameters:
      prompt - the prompt to use when asking for input
      Returns:
      the entered string this will represent a directory
      Throws:
      IOException - if there is a problem setting up the reader on the input or reading the input

    • getDirectory

      public static String getDirectory(String prompt, String defaultValue) throws IOException
      Asks the user to enter a string representing a directory which can be relative or absolute, ensures that the entered string represents an existing directory (not a file) and if it's not then requests another go, if the user enters an empty string then the defaultValue is used
      Parameters:
      prompt - the prompt to use when asking for input
      defaultValue - the default to use if the user just presses return
      Returns:
      the entered string this will represent a directory
      Throws:
      IOException - if there is a problem setting up the reader on the input or reading the input

    • listDirectoryNames

      public static List<String> listDirectoryNames(String dir)
      Locates all entries in the specified directory strict alpha order with directories first. Entries determined as hidden by the OS (e.g. starting with . in Unix / Linux / MacOS, hidden flag for windows) are removed from the result
      Parameters:
      dir - the directory to list
      Returns:
      returns the list of names

    • listDirectoryNames

      public static List<String> listDirectoryNames(String dir, boolean excludeHiddenFiles)
      Locates all entries in the specified directory strict alpha order with directories first
      Parameters:
      dir - the directory to list
      excludeHiddenFiles - if true then entries with as determined as hidden by the OS (e.g. starting with . in Unix / Linux / MacOS, hidden flag for windows) are removed from the list
      Returns:
      returns the list of names

    • listDirectoryNames

      public static List<String> listDirectoryNames(Path dir)
      Locates all entries in the specified directory strict alpha order with directories first. Entries determined as hidden by the OS (e.g. starting with . in Unix / Linux / MacOS, hidden flag for windows) are removed from the result
      Parameters:
      dir - the directory to list
      Returns:
      returns the list of names

    • listDirectoryNames

      public static List<String> listDirectoryNames(Path dir, boolean excludeHiddenFiles)
      Locates all entries in the specified directory strict alpha order with directories first
      Parameters:
      dir - the directory to list
      excludeHiddenFiles - if true then entries with as determined as hidden by the OS (e.g. starting with . in Unix / Linux / MacOS, hidden flag for windows) are removed from the list
      Returns:
      returns the list of names

    • listDirectoryNames

      public static List<String> listDirectoryNames(String dir, timgutilities.textio.DirectoryListFilterType listFilterType)
      Locates specified (dir or file or both) entries in the specified directory strict alpha order with directories first if they are in the output. Entries determined as hidden by the OS (e.g. starting with . in Unix / Linux / MacOS, hidden flag for windows) are removed from the result
      Parameters:
      dir - the directory to list
      listFilterType - what entries to return (sub dirs, files or both)
      Returns:
      the names of the type specified by the filter only
      See Also:
      • DirectoryListFilterType

    • listDirectoryNames

      public static List<String> listDirectoryNames(String dir, timgutilities.textio.DirectoryListFilterType listFilterType, boolean excludeHiddenFiles)
      Locates specified (dir or file or both) entries in the specified directory strict alpha order with directories first if they are in the output
      Parameters:
      dir - the directory to list
      listFilterType - what entries to return (sub dirs, files or both)
      excludeHiddenFiles - if true then entries with as determined as hidden by the OS (e.g. starting with . in Unix / Linux / MacOS, hidden flag for windows) are removed from the list
      Returns:
      the names of the type specified by the filter only
      See Also:
      • DirectoryListFilterType

    • listDirectoryNames

      public static List<String> listDirectoryNames(Path dir, timgutilities.textio.DirectoryListFilterType listFilterType)
      Locates specified (dir or file or both) entries in the specified directory strict alpha order with directories first if they are in the output. Entries determined as hidden by the OS (e.g. starting with . in Unix / Linux / MacOS, hidden flag for windows) are removed from the result
      Parameters:
      dir - the directory to list
      listFilterType - what entries to return (sub dirs, files or both)
      Returns:
      the names of the type specified by the filter only
      See Also:
      • DirectoryListFilterType

    • listDirectoryNames

      public static List<String> listDirectoryNames(Path dir, timgutilities.textio.DirectoryListFilterType listFilterType, boolean excludeHiddenFiles)
      Locates specified (dir or file or both) entries in the specified directory strict alpha order with directories first if they are in the output
      Parameters:
      dir - the directory to list
      listFilterType - what entries to return (sub dirs, files or both)
      excludeHiddenFiles - if true then entries with as determined as hidden by the OS (e.g. starting with . in Unix / Linux / MacOS, hidden flag for windows) are removed from the list
      Returns:
      the names only
      See Also:
      • DirectoryListFilterType

    • listDirectoryNames

      public static List<String> listDirectoryNames(String dir, timgutilities.textio.DirectoryListOrderType listOrderType)
      Locates all entries in the specified directory in output in either dir first, file first or mixed, returned in strict alpha order by grouping. Entries determined as hidden by the OS (e.g. starting with . in Unix / Linux / MacOS, hidden flag for windows) are removed from the result
      Parameters:
      dir - the directory to list
      listOrderType - how the resulting entries should be ordered
      Returns:
      the names only
      See Also:
      • DirectoryListOrderType

    • listDirectoryNames

      public static List<String> listDirectoryNames(String dir, timgutilities.textio.DirectoryListOrderType listOrderType, boolean excludeHiddenFiles)
      Locates all entries in the specified directory in output in either dir first, file first or mixed, returned in strict alpha order by grouping
      Parameters:
      dir - the directory to list
      listOrderType - how the resulting entries should be ordered
      excludeHiddenFiles - if true then entries with as determined as hidden by the OS (e.g. starting with . in Unix / Linux / MacOS, hidden flag for windows) are removed from the list
      Returns:
      the names only
      See Also:
      • DirectoryListOrderType

    • listDirectoryNames

      public static List<String> listDirectoryNames(Path dir, timgutilities.textio.DirectoryListOrderType listOrderType)
      Locates all entries in the specified directory in output in either dir first, file first or mixed, returned in strict alpha order by grouping. Entries determined as hidden by the OS (e.g. starting with . in Unix / Linux / MacOS, hidden flag for windows) are removed from the result
      Parameters:
      dir - the directory to list
      listOrderType - how the resulting entries should be ordered
      Returns:
      the names only
      See Also:
      • DirectoryListOrderType

    • listDirectoryNames

      public static List<String> listDirectoryNames(Path dir, timgutilities.textio.DirectoryListOrderType listOrderType, boolean excludeHiddenFiles)
      Locates all entries in the specified directory in output in either dir first, file first or mixed, returned in strict alpha order by grouping
      Parameters:
      dir - the directory to list
      listOrderType - how the resulting entries should be ordered
      excludeHiddenFiles - if true then entries with as determined as hidden by the OS (e.g. starting with . in Unix / Linux / MacOS, hidden flag for windows) are removed from the list
      Returns:
      the names only
      See Also:
      • DirectoryListOrderType

    • listDirectoryNames

      public static List<String> listDirectoryNames(String dir, timgutilities.textio.DirectoryListFilterType listFilterType, timgutilities.textio.DirectoryListOrderType listOrderType)
      Locates the specified entries (dir, file, both) in the specified directory, returning in strict alpha order with the specified grouping (dir / file first or mixed). Entries determined as hidden by the OS (e.g. starting with . in Unix / Linux / MacOS, hidden flag for windows) are removed from the result
      Parameters:
      dir - the directory to list
      listFilterType - what entries to return (sub dirs, files or both)
      listOrderType - how the resulting entries should be ordered
      Returns:
      the names only
      See Also:
      • DirectoryListFilterType
      • DirectoryListOrderType

      public static List<String> listDirectoryNames(String dir, timgutilities.textio.DirectoryListFilterType listFilterType, timgutilities.textio.DirectoryListOrderType listOrderType, boolean excludeHiddenFiles)
      Locates the specified entries (dir, file, both) in the specified directory, returning in strict alpha order with the specified grouping (dir / file first or mixed)
      Parameters:
      dir - the directory to list
      listFilterType - what entries to return (sub dirs, files or both)
      listOrderType - how the resulting entries should be ordered
      excludeHiddenFiles - if true then entries with as determined as hidden by the OS (e.g. starting with . in Unix / Linux / MacOS, hidden flag for windows) are removed from the list
      Returns:
      the names only
      See Also:
      • DirectoryListFilterType

    • listDirectoryNameTypes

      public static List<String> listDirectoryNameTypes(String dir)
      Locates all entries in the specified directory strict alpha order with directories first. Entries determined as hidden by the OS (e.g. starting with . in Unix / Linux / MacOS, hidden flag for windows) are removed from the result
      Parameters:
      dir - the directory to list
      Returns:
      list of name/type strings

    • listDirectoryNameTypes

      public static List<String> listDirectoryNameTypes(String dir, boolean excludeHiddenFiles)
      Locates all entries in the specified directory strict alpha order with directories first.
      Parameters:
      dir - the directory to list
      excludeHiddenFiles - if true then entries with as determined as hidden by the OS (e.g. starting with . in Unix / Linux / MacOS, hidden flag for windows) are removed from the list
      Returns:
      list of name/type strings

      Locates all entries in the specified directory in output in either dir first, file first or mixed, returned in strict alpha order by grouping. Entries determined as hidden by the OS (e.g. starting with . in Unix / Linux / MacOS, hidden flag for windows) are removed from the result
      Parameters:
      dir - the directory to list
      listOrderType - how the resulting entries should be ordered
      Returns:
      the names only

      listOrderType - how the resulting entries should be ordered
      excludeHiddenFiles - if true then entries with as determined as hidden by the OS (e.g. starting with . in Unix / Linux / MacOS, hidden flag for windows) are removed from the list
      Returns:
      the names only

      • DirectoryListOrderType

      public static List<String> listDirectoryNameTypes(Path dir, timgutilities.textio.DirectoryListFilterType listFilterType, timgutilities.textio.DirectoryListOrderType listOrderType, boolean excludeHiddenFiles)
      Locates the specified entries (dir, file, both) in the specified directory, returning in strict alpha order with the specified grouping (dir / file first or mixed).
      Parameters:
      dir - the directory to list
      listFilterType - what entries to return (sub dirs, files or both)
      listOrderType - how the resulting entries should be ordered
      excludeHiddenFiles - if true then entries with as determined as hidden by the OS (e.g. starting with . in Unix / Linux / MacOS, hidden flag for windows) are removed from the list
      Returns:

    • listDirectoryEntriesAsString

      public static List<String> listDirectoryEntriesAsString(String dir, timgutilities.textio.DirectoryListFilterType listFilterType, timgutilities.textio.DirectoryListOrderType listOrderType, String regexp, boolean regexpFilesOnly, boolean nameAndType)
      From the given directory list the directory entries as strings subject to the controls. Entries determined as hidden by the OS (e.g. starting with . in Unix / Linux / MacOS, hidden flag for windows) are removed from the result
      Parameters:
      dir - directory to list
      listFilterType - what entries to return (sub dirs, files or both)
      listOrderType - how the resulting entries should be ordered
      regexp - if provided is used to only select matching entries, for example files ending in .java
      regexpFilesOnly - does the regexp apply to both directories and files or just files
      nameAndType - if true then the name and type will be in the result (e.g. tim.txt (File)), if fals only the name (e.g. tim.txt)
      Returns:
      the names only or names + type (see nameAndType flag)
      See Also:
      • DirectoryListFilterType
      • DirectoryListOrderType

    • listDirectoryEntriesAsString

      public static List<String> listDirectoryEntriesAsString(Path dir, timgutilities.textio.DirectoryListFilterType listFilterType, timgutilities.textio.DirectoryListOrderType listOrderType, String regexp, boolean regexpFilesOnly, boolean nameAndType)
      From the given directory list the directory entries as strings subject to the controls. Entries determined as hidden by the OS (e.g. starting with . in Unix / Linux / MacOS, hidden flag for windows) are removed from the result
      Parameters:
      dir - directory to list
      listFilterType - what entries to return (sub dirs, files or both)
      listOrderType - how the resulting entries should be ordered
      regexp - if provided is used to only select matching entries, for example files ending in .java
      regexpFilesOnly - does the regexp apply to both directories and files or just files
      nameAndType - if true then the name and type will be in the result (e.g. tim.txt (File)), if fals only the name (e.g. tim.txt)
      Returns:
      the names only or names + type (see nameAndType flag)
      See Also:
      • DirectoryListFilterType
      • DirectoryListOrderType

    • listDirectoryEntries

      public static List<timgutilities.textio.DirectoryEntry> listDirectoryEntries(String dir, timgutilities.textio.DirectoryListFilterType listFilterType, timgutilities.textio.DirectoryListOrderType listOrderType, String regexp, boolean regexpFilesOnly)
      From the given directory list the directory contents as directory entries subject to the controls. Entries determined as hidden by the OS (e.g. starting with . in Unix / Linux / MacOS, hidden flag for windows) are removed from the result
      Parameters:
      dir - directory to list
      listFilterType - what entries to return (sub dirs, files or both)
      listOrderType - how the resulting entries should be ordered
      regexp - if provided is used to only select matching entries, for example files ending in .java
      regexpFilesOnly - does the regexp apply to both directories and files or just files
      Returns:
      list of matching entries ordered as requested
      See Also:
      • DirectoryListFilterType
      • DirectoryListOrderType

    • listDirectoryEntries

      public static List<timgutilities.textio.DirectoryEntry> listDirectoryEntries(Path dir, timgutilities.textio.DirectoryListFilterType listFilterType, timgutilities.textio.DirectoryListOrderType listOrderType, String regexp, boolean regexpFilesOnly)
      From the given directory list the directory contents as directory entries subject to the controls. Entries determined as hidden by the OS (e.g. starting with . in Unix / Linux / MacOS, hidden flag for windows) are removed from the result
      Parameters:
      dir - directory to list
      listFilterType - what entries to return (sub dirs, files or both)
      listOrderType - how the resulting entries should be ordered
      regexp - if provided is used to only select matching entries, for example files ending in .java
      regexpFilesOnly - does the regexp apply to both directories and files or just files
      Returns:
      list of matching entries ordered as requested

      • DirectoryListOrderType

    • listDirectoryEntries

      public static List<timgutilities.textio.DirectoryEntry> listDirectoryEntries(Path dir, timgutilities.textio.DirectoryListFilterType listFilterType, timgutilities.textio.DirectoryListOrderType listOrderType, boolean excludeHiddenFiles, String regexp, boolean regexpFilesOnly)
      From the given directory list the directory contents as directory entries subject to the controls.
      Parameters:
      dir - directory to list
      listFilterType - what entries to return (sub dirs, files or both)
      listOrderType - how the resulting entries should be ordered
      excludeHiddenFiles - if true then entries with as determined as hidden by the OS (e.g. starting with . in Unix / Linux / MacOS, hidden flag for windows) are removed from the list conventions) are not included in the list
      regexp - if provided is used to only select matching entries, for example files ending in .java
      regexpFilesOnly - does the regexp apply to both directories and files or just files
      Returns:
      list of matching entries ordered as requested
      See Also:
      • DirectoryListFilterType
      • DirectoryListOrderType

    • buildChoiceDescriptionDataFromDirectory

      public static timgutilities.textio.ChoiceDescriptionData<timgutilities.textio.DirectoryEntry> buildChoiceDescriptionDataFromDirectory(String dir, timgutilities.textio.DirectoryListFilterType listFilterType, timgutilities.textio.DirectoryListOrderType listOrderType, boolean excludeHiddenFiles, String regexp, boolean regexpFilesOnly, boolean nameAndType, boolean addAbandon, boolean addCurrentDirectory, boolean addParentDirectory)
      From the given directory list the directory entries as strings subject to the controls
      Parameters:
      dir - directory to list
      listFilterType - what entries to return (sub dirs, files or both)
      listOrderType - how the resulting entries should be ordered
      excludeHiddenFiles - if true then entries with as determined as hidden by the OS (e.g. starting with . in Unix / Linux / MacOS, hidden flag for windows) are removed from the list
      regexp - if provided is used to only select matching entries, for example files ending in .java
      regexpFilesOnly - does the regexp apply to both directories and files or just files
      nameAndType - if true then the name and type will be in the result (e.g. tim.txt (File)), if false only the name (e.g. tim.txt)
      addAbandon - If true then the abandon option will be added at the end of the list and made the default
      addCurrentDirectory - If true then an entry will be made for the current directory (".")
      addParentDirectory - If true then an entry will be made for the parent directory ("..")
      Returns:
      the ChoiceDescriptionData object which is ready to be used.
      See Also:
      • DirectoryListFilterType
      • DirectoryListOrderType

    • getCurrentDirectoryName

      public String getCurrentDirectoryName()
      getter for currentDirectoryName
      Returns:
      the value of currentDirectoryName

      public void setCurrentDirectoryName(String newCurrentDirectoryName)
      setter for currentDirectoryName Be very careful using this to ensure you don;t use a valid entry name
      Parameters:
      newCurrentDirectoryName - the new value for cparentDirectoryName

    • getParentDirectoryName

      public String getParentDirectoryName()
      getter for parentDirectoryName
      Returns:
      the value of parentDirectoryName

      public void setParentDirectoryName(String newParentDirectoryName)

      Parameters:

    • buildChoiceDescriptionDataFromDirectory

      public static timgutilities.textio.ChoiceDescriptionData<timgutilities.textio.DirectoryEntry> buildChoiceDescriptionDataFromDirectory(Path dir, timgutilities.textio.DirectoryListFilterType listFilterType, timgutilities.textio.DirectoryListOrderType listOrderType, boolean excludeHiddenFiles, String regexp, boolean regexpFilesOnly, boolean nameAndType, boolean addAbandon, boolean addCurrentDirectory, boolean addParentDirectory)
      From the given directory list the directory entries as strings subject to the controls
      Parameters:
      dir - directory to list
      listFilterType - what entries to return (sub dirs, files or both)
      listOrderType - how the resulting entries should be ordered
      excludeHiddenFiles - if true then entries with as determined as hidden by the OS (e.g. starting with . in Unix / Linux / MacOS, hidden flag for windows) are removed from the list
      regexp - if provided is used to only select matching entries, for example files ending in .java
      regexpFilesOnly - does the regexp apply to both directories and files or just files
      nameAndType - if true then the name and type will be in the result (e.g. tim.txt (File)), if false only the name (e.g. tim.txt)
      addAbandon - If true then the abandon option will be added at the end of the list and made the default
      addCurrentDirectory - If true then an entry will be made for the current directory (".") at the top of the list
      addParentDirectory - If true then an entry will be made for the parent directory ("..") at the top of the list
      Returns:
      the ChoiceDescriptionData object which is ready to be used.
      See Also:
      • DirectoryListFilterType
      • DirectoryListOrderType

    • choseFromDirectory

      public static timgutilities.textio.DirectoryEntry choseFromDirectory(String prompt, String dir, timgutilities.textio.DirectorySelectionMode selectionMode, timgutilities.textio.DirectoryListOrderType listOrderType, boolean excludeHiddenFiles, String regexp, boolean regexpFilesOnly, boolean nameAndType, boolean addAbandon) throws IOException
      From the given directory list the directory entries as strings subject to the controls Entries shown will depend on the selectionMode, so if the selection mode limits choices to directories only directories will be shown, but a mode that allows only files to be chosen will display the directories for navigation purposes, but they cannot be chosen. Note that for the selection Modes that support navigation it is not possible to navigate above the initially provided start directory, though navigation back up to it is allowed.
      Parameters:
      prompt - the prompt to use when asking for input
      dir - directory to list
      selectionMode - what choices to allow, this will influence what content is chosen and thus indirectly may override sort order for some options (e.g. if only files can be selected then no subdirectories will be shown)
      listOrderType - how the resulting entries should be ordered
      excludeHiddenFiles - if true then entries with as determined as hidden by the OS (e.g. starting with . in Unix / Linux / MacOS, hidden flag for windows) are removed from the list
      regexp - if provided is used to only select matching entries, for example files ending in .java
      regexpFilesOnly - does the regexp apply to both directories and files or just files
      nameAndType - if true then the name and type will be in the result (e.g. tim.txt (File)), if false only the name (e.g. tim.txt)
      addAbandon - If true then the abandon option will be added at the end of the list and made the default
      Returns:
      the DirectoryEntry selected or null if the abandon choice was taken
      Throws:
      IOException - if there is a problem setting up the reader on the input or reading the input
      See Also:
      • DirectoryEntry
      • DirectorySelectionMode
      • DirectoryListOrderType

    • choseFromDirectory

      public static timgutilities.textio.DirectoryEntry choseFromDirectory(String prompt, Path dir, timgutilities.textio.DirectorySelectionMode selectionMode, timgutilities.textio.DirectoryListOrderType listOrderType, boolean excludeHiddenFiles, String regexp, boolean regexpFilesOnly, boolean nameAndType, boolean addAbandon) throws IOException
      From the given directory list the directory entries as strings subject to the controls. Entries shown will depend on the selectionMode, so if the selection mode limits choices to directories only directories will be shown, but a mode that allows only files to be chosen will display the directories for navigation purposes, but they cannot be chosen. Note that for the selection Modes that support navigation it is not possible to navigate above the initially provided start directory, though navigation back up to it is allowed.
      Parameters:
      prompt - the prompt to use when asking for input
      dir - directory to list
      selectionMode - what choices to allow, this will influence what content is chosen and thus indirectly may override sort order for some options (e.g. if only files can be selected then no subdirectories will be shown)
      listOrderType - how the resulting entries should be ordered
      excludeHiddenFiles - if true then entries with as determined as hidden by the OS (e.g. starting with . in Unix / Linux / MacOS, hidden flag for windows) are removed from the list
      regexp - if provided is used to only select matching entries, for example files ending in .java
      regexpFilesOnly - does the regexp apply to both directories and files or just files
      nameAndType - if true then the name and type will be in the result (e.g. tim.txt (File)), if false only the name (e.g. tim.txt)
      addAbandon - If true then the abandon option will be added at the end of the list and made the default
      Returns:
      the DirectoryEntry selected or null if the abandon choice was taken
      Throws:
      IOException - if there is a problem setting up the reader on the input or reading the input.
      See Also:
      • DirectoryEntry
      • DirectorySelectionMode
      • DirectoryListOrderType

    • getISODateTimeTimeZone

      public static String getISODateTimeTimeZone(String prompt) throws IOException
      ask for the date / time with a timezone requested by name and offering a default of the current TZ
      Parameters:
      prompt - the prompt to use when asking for input
      Returns:
      a string representing the entered date time and tz
      Throws:
      IOException - if there is a problem setting up the reader on the input or reading the input

    • getISODateTime

      public static String getISODateTime(String prompt) throws IOException
      Asks the user to enter a data and time (but no time zone) returns a string in the format YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm Attempts to ensure that the entered data is valid (e.g. that the day is only allowed values for the month, so 1-30 for april, 1-31 for March and either 1-28 or 1-29 for Feb. Only works on the Gregorian calendar The UI will use the current time / date as the default values
      Parameters:
      prompt - the prompt to use when asking for input
      Returns:
      a string representing the entered date and time
      Throws:
      IOException - if there is a problem setting up the reader on the input or reading the input

    • getISODateTime

      public static String getISODateTime(String prompt, boolean askForTimezone) throws IOException
      Asks the user to enter a data and time (but no time zone) returns a string in the format YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm if askForTimezone is true then prompts for the timezone to use e.g. Europe/London and offers a default of the current TZ The UI will use the current time / date as the default values
      Parameters:
      prompt - the prompt to use when asking for input
      askForTimezone - if true the user will be asked to select a timezone
      Returns:
      a string representing the entered data time and tz
      Throws:
      IOException - if there is a problem setting up the reader on the input or reading the input

    • getISOLocalDateTime

      public static LocalDateTime getISOLocalDateTime(String prompt) throws IOException
      Asks the user to enter a data and time (but no time zone) returns a string in the format YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm if askForTimezone is true then prompts for the timezone to use e.g. Europe/London and offers a default of the current TZ The UI will use the current time / date as the default values
      Parameters:
      prompt - the prompt to use when asking for input
      Returns:
      The entered data time, note this is local so there is no timezone
      Throws:
      IOException - if there is a problem setting up the reader on the input or reading the input

    • getISODateTimeTimeZone

      public static String getISODateTimeTimeZone(String prompt, boolean askForTimezone, boolean askForTimezoneName, boolean defaultToCurrentTimezone) throws IOException
      Asks the user to enter a data and time (in whatever TZ) and returns a string in the format YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:SS If you are using a timezone then the time entered should be in the local time for that timezone, not that for some name based timezones e.g. Europe/Paris then this also allows for summer time / winter time adjustments (not all TZ support daylight savings, for example the ones starting with GMT) So for example entering 2023-01-01 12:00:00 with timezone Europe/London returns an ISO formatted string with no offset, however 2023-06-01 12:00:00 with a TRZ fo Europe/London would return a formatted string with an offset of +01:00 as that time is a summer time and so one hour ahead if askForTimezone is true then prompt for the timezone if askForTimezoneName is true prompts for a timezone by name, e.g. Europe/London if askForTimezoneName is false then ask an offset from GMT in terms of hours an mins, the resulting string will be YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mmZ01:00 (in the case of british summer time which is 1 hours ahead of GMT, or YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mmZ04:30) for India which is 4 1/2 hours ahead. if defaulToCurrentTimezone is true then will prompt for the timezone based on the current timezone name / offset The UI will use the current time / date as the default values If defaultToCurrentOffsett is true then the current offser in hours and mins will be offered as the TZ offset hours and mins
      Parameters:
      prompt - the prompt to use when asking for input
      askForTimezone - if true the user will ba asked for a timezone
      askForTimezoneName - if asking for timezone they will be asked by name, if false by offset
      defaultToCurrentTimezone - if trie the timezone will default to the current if they user just presses return
      Returns:
      a string representing the entered date time and tz
      Throws:
      IOException - if there is a problem setting up the reader on the input or reading the input

    • getISODateTimeTimeZone

      public static String getISODateTimeTimeZone(String prompt, boolean askForTimezone, boolean askForTimezoneName, boolean defaultToCurrentTimezone, LocalDateTime mindtg, LocalDateTime maxdtg) throws IOException
      Asks the user to enter a data and time (in whatever TZ) and returns a string in the format YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:SS If you are using a timezone then the time entered should be in the local time for that timezone, not that for some name based timezones e.g. Europe/Paris then this also allows for summer time / winter time adjustments (not all TZ support daylight savings, for example the ones starting with GMT) So for example entering 2023-01-01 12:00:00 with timezone Europe/London returns an ISO formatted string with no offset, however 2023-06-01 12:00:00 with a TRZ fo Europe/London would return a formatted string with an offset of +01:00 as that time is a summer time and so one hour ahead if askForTimezone is true then prompt for the timezone if askForTimezoneName is true prompts for a timezone by name, e.g. Europe/London if askForTimezoneName is false then ask an offset from GMT in terms of hours an mins, the resulting string will be YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mmZ01:00 (in the case of british summer time which is 1 hours ahead of GMT, or YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mmZ04:30) for India which is 4 1/2 hours ahead. if defaulToCurrentTimezone is true then will prompt for the timezone based on the current timezone name / offset The UI will use the current time / date as the default values If defaultToCurrentOffsett is true then the current offser in hours and mins will be offered as the TZ offset hours and mins
      Parameters:
      prompt - the prompt to use when asking for input
      askForTimezone - if true the user will ba asked for a timezone
      askForTimezoneName - if asking for timezone they will be asked by name, if false by offset
      defaultToCurrentTimezone - if trie the timezone will default to the current if they user just presses return
      mindtg - the entered info must be >= this
      maxdtg - the entered info must be <= this
      Returns:
      a string representing the entered date time and tz
      Throws:
      IOException - if there is a problem setting up the reader on the input or reading the input

    • getISOZonedDateTime

      public static ZonedDateTime getISOZonedDateTime(String prompt, boolean askForTimezone, boolean askForTimezoneName, boolean defaultToCurrentTimezone, LocalDateTime mindtg, LocalDateTime maxdtg) throws IOException
      Asks the user to enter a data and time (in the local or whatever TZ they enter) in the format YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:SS If you are asking for a timezone then the time entered should be in the local time for that timezone, not that for some name based timezones e.g. Europe/Paris then this also allows for summer time / winter time adjustments (not all TZ support daylight savings, for example the ones starting with GMT) So for example entering 2023-01-01 12:00:00 with timezone Europe/London returns an ISO formatted string with no offset, however 2023-06-01 12:00:00 with a TRZ fo Europe/London would return a formatted string with an offset of +01:00 as that time is a summer time and so one hour ahead if askForTimezone is true then prompt for the timezone if askForTimezone is false then the current (i.e. system local) TZ is used. if askForTimezoneName is true prompts for a timezone by name, e.g. Europe/London if askForTimezoneName is false then ask an offset from GMT in terms of hours an mins, the resulting string will be YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mmZ01:00 (in the case of british summer time which is 1 hours ahead of GMT, or YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mmZ04:30) for India which is 4 1/2 hours ahead. if defaulToCurrentTimezone is true then will prompt for the timezone based on the current timezone name / offset The UI will use the current time / date as the default values If defaultToCurrentOffsett is true then the current offser in hours and mins will be offered as the TZ offset hours and mins
      Parameters:
      prompt - the prompt to use when asking for input
      askForTimezone - if true the user will ba asked for a timezone
      askForTimezoneName - if asking for timezone they will be asked by name, if false by offset
      defaultToCurrentTimezone - if true the timezone will default to the current if they user just presses return
      mindtg - the entered info must be >= this
      maxdtg - the entered info must be <= this
      Returns:
      a string representing the entered date time and tz
      Throws:
      IOException - if there is a problem setting up the reader on the input or reading the input

    • getISODateTimeTimeZone

      public static String getISODateTimeTimeZone(String prompt, ZoneId zoneId) throws IOException
      Asks the user to enter a full date, time and in the specified time zone
      Parameters:
      prompt - the prompt to use when asking for input
      zoneId - the timezone to use
      Returns:
      a string representing the entered date time and tz
      Throws:
      IOException - if there is a problem setting up the reader on the input or reading the input

    • getISODateTimeTimeZone

      public static String getISODateTimeTimeZone(String prompt, ZoneId zoneId, LocalDateTime mindtg, LocalDateTime maxdtg) throws IOException
      Asks the user to enter the data and time in the specified timezone where the entered data must be >= mindtg and <= maxdtg
      Parameters:
      prompt - the prompt to use when asking for input
      zoneId - the timezone to use
      mindtg - the entered info must be >= this
      maxdtg - the entered info must be <= this
      Returns:
      a string representing the entered date time and tz
      Throws:
      IOException - if there is a problem setting up the reader on the input or reading the input

    • getISOZonedDateTimeTimeZone

      public static ZonedDateTime getISOZonedDateTimeTimeZone(String prompt, ZoneId zoneId, LocalDateTime mindtg, LocalDateTime maxdtg) throws IOException
      Asks the user to enter the data and time in the specified timezone where the entered data must be >= mindtg and <= maxdtg
      Parameters:
      prompt - the prompt to use when asking for input
      zoneId - the timezone to use
      mindtg - the entered info must be >= this
      maxdtg - the entered info must be <= this
      Returns:
      a string representing the entered date time and tz
      Throws:
      IOException - if there is a problem setting up the reader on the input or reading the input

    • getISOTimeZoneOffset

      public static String getISOTimeZoneOffset(String prompt) throws IOException
      prompts for an offset from GMT in terms of hours an mins, the resulting string will be 01:00 (in the case of british summer time which is 1 hours ahead of GMT, or 04:30) for India which is 4 1/2 hours ahead. The UI will use gmt as the default values
      Parameters:
      prompt - the prompt to use when asking for input
      Returns:
      the entered offset (default to the current offset if the user just presses return)
      Throws:
      IOException - if there is a problem setting up the reader on the input or reading the input

    • getISOTimeZoneOffset

      public static String getISOTimeZoneOffset(String prompt, boolean defaulToCurrentOffset) throws IOException
      prompts for an offset from GMT in terms of hours an mins, the resulting string will be 01:00 (in the case of british summer time which is 1 hours ahead of GMT, or 04:30) for India which is 4 1/2 hours ahead. The UI will use gmt as the default values If defaultToCurrentOffsett is true then the current offset in hours and mins will be offered as the TZ offset hours and mins
      Parameters:
      prompt - the prompt to use when asking for input
      defaulToCurrentOffset - if true default to the current offset if the user just presses return
      Returns:
      the entered offset
      Throws:
      IOException - if there is a problem setting up the reader on the input or reading the input

    • getISOOffsetTimeZoneOffset

      public static ZoneOffset getISOOffsetTimeZoneOffset(String prompt, boolean defaulToCurrentOffset) throws IOException
      prompts for an offset from GMT in terms of hours an mins, the resulting string will be 01:00 (in the case of british summer time which is 1 hours ahead of GMT, or 04:30) for India which is 4 1/2 hours ahead. The UI will use gmt as the default values If defaultToCurrentOffsett is true then the current offset in hours and mins will be offered as the TZ offset hours and mins
      Parameters:
      prompt - the prompt to use when asking for input
      defaulToCurrentOffset - if true default to the current offset if the user just presses return
      Returns:
      the entered offset
      Throws:
      IOException - if there is a problem setting up the reader on the input or reading the input

      public static String getTimeZoneNameByName(String prompt, boolean defaulToCurrentTimeZone) throws IOException
      Gets the name of a timezone
      Parameters:

      defaulToCurrentTimeZone - if true will offer the current tx as a default if the user presses return
      Returns:
      a string of the selected tx
      Throws:
      IOException - if there is a problem setting up the reader on the input or reading the input

      public static ZoneId getTimeZoneByName(String prompt, boolean defaulToCurrentTimeZone) throws IOException

      Parameters:
      prompt - the prompt to use when asking for input
      defaulToCurrentTimeZone - if true default to the current timezone if the user just presses return
      Returns:
      the entered offset
      Throws:

      public static String getISOTime(String prompt) throws IOException

      Parameters:

      Returns:
      the entred time
      Throws:
      IOException - if there is a problem setting up the reader on the input or reading the input

      maxtime - the entered time must be <= this

    • getISODate

      public static String getISODate(String prompt) throws IOException
      Prompts the user to enter a date with any allowed date, the current local date is used as the default. The day of the month will be restricted to the actual number of days in that month in that year (so for Feb 28 normally, but 28 in leap years The return string is of the format YYYY-MM-DD
      Parameters:
      prompt - the prompt to use when asking for input
      Returns:
      the entered date
      Throws:
      IOException - if there is a problem setting up the reader on the input or reading the input

    • getISODate

      public static String getISODate(String prompt, LocalDate mindate, LocalDate maxdate) throws IOException
      Prompts the user to enter a date, which the current local date is used as the default. The day of the month will be restricted to the actual number of days in that month in that year (so for Seb 28 normally, but 28 in leap years The return string is of the format YYYY-MM-DD
      Parameters:
      prompt - the prompt to use when asking for input
      mindate - the entered date must be >= this
      maxdate - the entered date must be <= this
      Returns:
      the entered date
      Throws:
      IOException - if there is a problem setting up the reader on the input or reading the input

      Parameters:

    • buildChoiceDescriptionDataFromSampleEnumValue

      public static <T extends Enum<T>> timgutilities.textio.ChoiceDescriptionData<Enum<T>> buildChoiceDescriptionDataFromSampleEnumValue(Enum<T> enumconstant)
      Given an enum constant builds a choice description data for all the values of the enum class, setting the specific value of the enum constant as the default
      Type Parameters:
      T - the enum class
      Parameters:
      enumconstant - the enum value to use to determine the enum and the default
      Returns:
      the generated ChoiceDescriptionData

    • buildChoiceDescriptionDataFromSampleEnumValue

      public static <T extends Enum<T>> timgutilities.textio.ChoiceDescriptionData<Enum<T>> buildChoiceDescriptionDataFromSampleEnumValue(Enum<T> enumconstant, boolean setAsDefault)
      Given an enum constant builds a choice description data for all the values of the enum class, if setAsDefault is true setting the specific value of the enum constant as the default
      Type Parameters:
      T - the enum class
      Parameters:
      enumconstant - the enum value to use to determine the enum and the default
      setAsDefault - if true a default entry is set for the enum value, if false no default is created
      Returns:
      the generated ChoiceDescriptionData

    • buildChoiceDescriptionDataFromEnumClass

      public static <T extends Enum<T>> timgutilities.textio.ChoiceDescriptionData<Enum<T>> buildChoiceDescriptionDataFromEnumClass(Class<? extends Enum<T>> enumclass)
      Given the enum class build a ChoiceDescriptionData representing all of the constants in the enum
      Type Parameters:
      T - The class of the enum
      Parameters:
      enumclass - The class of the enum
      Returns:
      the generated ChoiceDescriptionData

    • buildChoiceDescriptionDataFromEnumValues

      public static <T extends Enum<T>> timgutilities.textio.ChoiceDescriptionData<Enum<T>> buildChoiceDescriptionDataFromEnumValues(Enum<T>[] enumconstants)
      Given an array of possible the enum constants build a ChoiceDescriptionData representing the supplied values
      Type Parameters:
      T - The class of the enum
      Parameters:
      enumconstants - An array of enum constants
      Returns:
      the generated ChoiceDescriptionData
